hero_subtitle: SFTY structures a secure, audit-ready compliance operating layer that supports institutional governance, role-based access, and traceable evidence for regulated digital asset businesses.

problem_title: Procurement slows without a clear compliance model
problem_description: Enterprise teams face uncertainty when compliance workflows lack defined governance and audit controls. This complicates procurement decisions, exposes audit readiness gaps and increases operational risk in regulated digital asset environments.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY structures role-based access, audit logs, evidence integrity and governance controls into one traceable compliance layer designed to reduce procurement risk and support enterprise readiness for VASPs.
workflow_title: From access controls to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY structures role-based permissions, audit logs and evidence retention into a controlled workflow that supports secure compliance and procurement readiness.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another compliance model. One audit-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides a provider-neutral compliance layer that links policies, decisions and evidence to reduce procurement risk and support audit-ready enterprise deployment.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY provides VASPs with a robust compliance layer that supports audit-ready evidence, role-based access, and enterprise deployment suitable for procurement and bank due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP procurement teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a procurement-safe compliance model that supports regulated VASPs with governance, auditability, secure deployment, and enterprise trust for structured compliance operations across multiple jurisdictions.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support audit-ready evidence?
faq_2_answer: SFTY preserves evidence history, decision logs, case records and audit trails to support compliance teams maintain clear, defensible records for regulatory, bank due diligence and internal inspection readiness.
faq_3_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_3_answer: SFTY connects and orchestrates existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, integrating their outputs into a connected compliance workflow without disrupting current tools or processes.
faq_4_question: How does role-based access governance work?
faq_4_answer: SFTY applies role-based access control with configurable permissions, audit logs and segregation of duties to secure sensitive data and support only authorized users can access or modify compliance cases.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY acts as a neutral control layer above existing compliance providers, turning their fragmented outputs into structured workflows, cases, decisions and reports without replacing the underlying systems.
final_title: Review secure enterprise compliance with SFTY
final_description: Explore how SFTY supports role-based access, audit logs, governance, and traceable evidence for procurement-safe compliance.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
